title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) - Easy Recipe

authentic Turkish red lentil soup recipe
best Turkish lentil soup at home
seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
h2Headings: What is Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)?
Step-by-Step: How to Make Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi
Serving Suggestions for Your Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh mint.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a warm bowl of authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi), a comforting and flavorful staple.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, cherished for its comforting warmth and rich, savory flavor. This traditional recipe combines simple ingredients like red lentils, tomatoes, and aromatic spices to create a velvety smooth and incredibly satisfying dish. Often served with a drizzle of spicy mint-infused butter, it's a staple in Turkish homes and a perfect starter for any meal.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. Chop the onion finely and mince the garlic.

instructions.title: Saute Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Build Flavor Base
instructions.text: Stir in the all-purpose flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly to create a roux. Add the tomato paste and cook for another minute, stirring to combine well with the onion mixture.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Add the rinsed lentils to the pot, followed by the chicken or vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend to Smoothness
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare Butter Sauce
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the unsalted butter over medium-low heat. Once the butter is melted and slightly foaming, add the dried mint and pul biber (red pepper flakes). Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring until fragrant, being careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a generous amount of the spiced butter sauce over each serving. Serve immediately with a wedge of lemon and crusty bread if desired.

tips: Always rinse your red lentils thoroughly until the water runs clear to ensure a clean taste and prevent excessive foam during cooking.
For the most authentic flavor, use a good quality Turkish tomato paste (salca) which has a rich, concentrated tomato flavor.
Do not skip the spiced butter topping (nane yagli sos); it is a signature element of Mercimek Corbasi that adds an incredible aroma and depth of flavor.
article:
Embrace the warmth and comfort of Turkish cuisine with a bowl of Mercimek Corbasi, the quintessential Turkish Red Lentil Soup. This vibrant, nutritious, and incredibly flavorful soup is a staple in Turkish households, gracing tables from bustling city restaurants to cozy family dinners. Known for its smooth texture, rich aroma, and a delightful kick from a spiced butter drizzle, Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a hug in a bowl, offering both sustenance and a taste of Anatolian hospitality. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a beginner in the kitchen, mastering this simple yet profound dish will open up a world of authentic Turkish flavors.

## What is Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-MEK chor-ba-SUH," translates directly to "lentil soup." While there are many variations of lentil soups globally, the Turkish version is distinct for its use of red lentils, which cook down to a beautifully creamy consistency, and its signature finishing drizzle of hot butter infused with dried mint and red pepper flakes. It’s a dish deeply ingrained in Turkish culture, often served as a comforting starter to any meal, a light lunch, or a hearty breakfast. Its popularity stems from its affordability, ease of preparation, and the abundance of red lentils in the region.

The soup's base is typically simple: red lentils, onions, garlic, and tomato paste, all simmered together until tender. The magic happens when these ingredients are blended into a silky-smooth purée, creating a velvety texture that is both satisfying and incredibly palatable. The distinct flavor profile is savory with a subtle earthiness from the lentils, brightened by the tomato paste and a hint of tang from a squeeze of fresh lemon juice added just before serving. It’s a testament to how humble ingredients can be transformed into something truly extraordinary, making Turkish Red Lentil Soup a beloved dish across the country and beyond.

## Ingredients for Mercimek Corbasi

The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its simplicity. You won't need an extensive shopping list to create this authentic Turkish delight. Here are the core ingredients that bring this soup to life:

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show! Make sure they are split red lentils, as they cook faster and break down more easily, contributing to the soup's signature creamy texture.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation. Finely diced, they provide depth and a sweet-savory base.
*   **Tomato Paste:** Adds a crucial layer of umami and a lovely reddish hue to the soup. Look for good quality Turkish tomato paste if available.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** Used for sautéing the aromatics and forming the base of the soup. Butter is also essential for the finishing drizzle.
*   **Water or Vegetable Broth:** The liquid component. Broth will add more flavor, but water works perfectly well, especially with the other robust ingredients.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** Essential seasonings to bring out all the flavors.
*   **Dried Mint (Nane):** A non-negotiable for the finishing drizzle. Its fresh, aromatic quality perfectly complements the earthiness of the lentils.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber):** Adds a mild to moderate heat and vibrant color to the minty butter drizzle. Adjust to your spice preference.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** For serving. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice is crucial for balancing the flavors and brightening the soup.

Optional additions can include a small amount of flour for thickening (though often not needed with red lentils), or a pinch of cumin for an extra layer of warmth.

## Step-by-Step: How to Make Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Making Mercimek Corbasi is a straightforward process, perfect for a comforting meal any day of the week.

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse 1 cup of red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy. Drain well.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add 1 finely diced medium onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2-3 cloves of minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Add Tomato Paste and Spices:** Stir in 1-2 tablespoons of tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. This step "cooks out" the raw flavor of the tomato paste and deepens its richness. Add 1 teaspoon of salt and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper. You can also add a pinch of dried mint or cumin here if desired.
4.  **Combine Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed lentils to the pot and stir to coat them with the onion and tomato mixture. Pour in 6 cups of water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Blend Until Smooth:** Remove the pot from the heat. Carefully use an immersion blender to purée the soup directly in the pot until it's completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, you can transfer the soup in batches to a standard blender (be very careful with hot liquids, filling the blender only halfway and holding the lid down with a towel) and then return it to the pot. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Taste and adjust seasonings if necessary.
6.  **Prepare the Mint Butter Drizzle (Nane Yağı):** In a small pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium-low heat. Once the butter is melted and slightly foamy, remove it from the heat and stir in 1-2 teaspoons of dried mint and 1 teaspoon of red pepper flakes (or more, to taste). The residual heat of the butter will toast the spices and release their aroma.
7.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Turkish Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Drizzle a generous spoonful of the spiced mint butter over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges and crusty bread.

## Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi

Achieving the perfect bowl of Mercimek Corbasi is simple, but a few key tips can elevate your soup from good to unforgettable:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils Thoroughly:** This is crucial. Rinsing removes starches and impurities, leading to a clearer, cleaner-tasting soup and preventing a gummy texture.
*   **Sauté Aromatics Well:** Don't rush the onion and garlic. Cooking them until soft and fragrant builds a foundational flavor for the soup.
*   **Cook the Tomato Paste:** Heating the tomato paste for a few minutes before adding liquid deepens its flavor and color, removing any raw or metallic taste.
*   **Achieve Silky Smoothness:** A good immersion blender is your best friend here. Blend until there are no visible lentil pieces left. This is a hallmark of authentic Mercimek Corbasi.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Red lentils absorb a lot of liquid. If your soup thickens too much after blending or sitting, simply whisk in a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your preferred consistency.
*   **Don't Skip the Mint Butter Drizzle:** This "nane yağı" (mint oil) is what truly distinguishes Turkish Red Lentil Soup. The fragrant mint and subtle heat from the pepper flakes add an incredible layer of flavor and aroma that is absolutely essential.
*   **Always Serve with Lemon:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the table isn't just a garnish; it's a vital flavor enhancer. It brightens the soup, cuts through the richness, and brings all the flavors into perfect balance.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Lentil Soup

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways:

*   **Traditional Starter:** In Turkey, it's often served as the first course of a larger meal, providing a warm and inviting opening.
*   **Light Lunch:** Paired with a slice of crusty Turkish bread (ekmek) or pita, it makes for a satisfying and nutritious light lunch.
*   **Hearty Breakfast:** It's not uncommon to find Mercimek Corbasi served for breakfast in Turkish homes, especially during colder months, offering a protein-rich start to the day.
*   **Accompaniments:** Always serve with plenty of lemon wedges for squeezing. A sprinkle of additional red pepper flakes, a dash of sumac, or a few fresh parsley leaves can also be added for extra flavor and visual appeal. Pickled vegetables (turşu) or a simple side salad with a light vinaigrette can also complement the soup beautifully.
*   **Yogurt Swirl:** For an extra creamy and tangy touch, some enjoy a dollop of plain yogurt swirled into their bowl, especially if they prefer a milder spice level.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Turkish Red Lentil Soup recipe is perfect as is, you can certainly explore minor variations to suit your taste or dietary needs:

*   **Added Vegetables:** For extra nutrition and body, you can sauté finely diced carrots or potatoes along with the onions and garlic. They will cook down with the lentils and blend seamlessly into the soup.
*   **Spice Profile:** While dried mint and red pepper flakes are traditional, you can experiment with a pinch of cumin for a warmer, earthier note, or a tiny bit of turmeric for color and a subtle health boost.
*   **Broth Choice:** While water is commonly used, vegetable broth will enhance the overall savory depth of the soup. Chicken broth can also be used if you're not keeping it vegetarian.
*   **Vegan/Dairy-Free:** This soup is easily made vegan. Simply use olive oil instead of butter for sautéing and for the finishing drizzle. The result is just as delicious and comforting.
*   **Garnish Alternatives:** Instead of just dried mint and red pepper flakes, consider a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or cilantro for a burst of fresh flavor, or a pinch of sumac for an extra lemony tang.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A tiny pinch of smoked paprika added with the tomato paste can introduce a subtle smoky undertone.

Mercimek Corbasi is a testament to the power of simple ingredients creating profound flavors. Its warmth, comforting texture, and balanced taste make it a beloved dish that truly embodies the heart of Turkish cuisine. Give this recipe a try, and you'll soon understand why this vibrant Turkish Red Lentil Soup holds such a special place on tables around the world.
